notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 3, Book 13 source file.
Strong Reader card: longer text and more inferential fable work.

book.title: The Milkmaid and Her Pail
book.description: The Milkmaid and Her Pail includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: longer text and more inferential fable work

weeks.title: The Milkmaid and Her Pail Book Card
vocabulary: 1. diligently - working hard and carefully; with great effort and attention
2. exceptional - unusually good; much better than normal
1. entrepreneurial - related to starting and running a business
2. accumulated - gathered or collected over time
1. grandiose - trying to seem very impressive and important
2. aristocratic - belonging to the highest social class; very elegant and refined
1. dignitaries - important people who hold high positions
2. rapturously - with intense joy and excitement
1. precariously - in an unsteady way; likely to fall or fail
1. assumptions - things believed to be true without proof
2. invaluable - extremely useful and important; worth more than money
journal: Write about why planning is helpful but daydreaming can be risky.
Explain what the milkmaid learns by the end of the story.
writing: Write about why planning is helpful but daydreaming can be risky.
Explain what the milkmaid learns by the end of the story.
questions: 1. What was Molly known for throughout the village?
2. How did Molly carry her pail of milk?
1. What did Molly plan to buy first with her milk money?
2. What was Molly's ultimate goal with her business plans?
1. How did Molly imagine herself in the future?
2. What kind of social events did Molly dream of attending?
1. What happened to Molly as she became lost in her fantasies?
2. How did Molly change her posture while daydreaming?
1. What caused the pail to fall from Molly's head?
2. What happened to all of Molly's plans when the milk spilled?
1. What important lesson did Molly learn from her experience?
2. How did Molly change her approach to work after this experience?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
